get.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific wallet including all addresses.

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description: Retrieves all addresses (accounts) associated with a specific wallet.

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.summary: Get all addresses for a wallet

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid input parameters

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

post.description: Creates metadata configuration for a token swap application. Currently supports LFJ and Pangolin protocols. The generated metadata can be used to create mini-apps for token swaps on supported blockchain networks.

LFJ Swap.description: Swap AVAX to JOE using LFJ protocol on Avalanche

Pangolin Swap.description: Swap AVAX to PNG using Pangolin protocol on Avalanche

metadata.description: Generated metadata object containing all swap configuration

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid parameters provided

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

403.description: Forbidden - Insufficient permissions. Requires INTERNAL or INTEGRATOR scope (ADMIN scope also allowed)

500.description: Internal Server Error - Failed to generate metadata

post.description: Creates metadata configuration for transferring native tokens across different blockchain networks. Converts native currency from source chain to native currency on destination chain (e.g., AVAX → ETH, AVAX → MATIC). This is useful for creating mini-apps that enable users to send native tokens from one chain and receive native tokens on another chain.

requestBody.description: Parameters for generating crosschain native token transfer metadata

value.title: Transfer AVAX to ETH
value.description: Transfer AVAX from Avalanche and receive ETH on Ethereum

Transfer AVAX to Ethereum (receive ETH).description: Send AVAX from Avalanche (43114) and receive ETH on Ethereum (1)

value.title: Transfer AVAX to MATIC
value.description: Transfer AVAX from Avalanche and receive MATIC on Polygon

Transfer AVAX to Polygon (receive MATIC).description: Send AVAX from Avalanche (43114) and receive MATIC on Polygon (137)

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid parameters provided (e.g., invalid chain IDs, missing required fields)

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

403.description: Forbidden - Insufficient permissions. Requires INTERNAL or INTEGRATOR scope (ADMIN scope also allowed)

500.description: Internal Server Error - Failed to generate metadata

post.summary: Generate metadata for Crosschain Transfer application

post.description: Creates metadata configuration for transferring native tokens (like AVAX, ETH, MATIC) on a single blockchain network. This endpoint generates metadata for simple native token transfers within the same chain, useful for creating mini-apps that enable users to send native tokens to specific addresses.

requestBody.description: Parameters for generating native token transfer metadata

value.title: Transfer AVAX
value.description: Send AVAX to another address on Avalanche network

value.title: Transfer ETH
value.description: Send ETH to another address on Ethereum network

200.description: Native token transfer metadata generated successfully

metadata.description: Generated metadata for native token transfer

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid parameters provided (e.g., invalid chain ID, invalid address format)

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

403.description: Forbidden - Insufficient permissions. Requires INTERNAL or INTEGRATOR scope (ADMIN scope also allowed)

500.description: Internal Server Error - Failed to generate metadata

post.summary: Generate metadata for Native Token Transfer application

post.description: Creates metadata configuration for bridging assets between different blockchain networks. This endpoint generates metadata for cross-chain bridge operations, allowing users to move assets (tokens) from one blockchain to another. Useful for creating mini-apps that enable cross-chain asset transfers.

requestBody.description: Parameters for generating crosschain bridge metadata

value.title: Bridge USDC
value.description: Bridge USDC from Avalanche to Arbitrum

Bridge from Avalanche to Arbitrum.description: Bridge USDC from Avalanche (43114) to Arbitrum (42161)

value.title: Bridge ETH
value.description: Bridge ETH from Ethereum to Polygon

Bridge from Ethereum to Polygon.description: Bridge ETH from Ethereum (1) to Polygon (137)

metadata.description: Generated metadata for crosschain bridge operation

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid bridge parameters (e.g., invalid chain IDs, missing required fields)

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

403.description: Forbidden - Insufficient permissions. Requires INTERNAL or INTEGRATOR scope (ADMIN scope also allowed)

500.description: Internal Server Error - Failed to generate bridge metadata

post.summary: Generate metadata for Crosschain Bridge application

post.description: Get a quote for swapping tokens on Uniswap V4. Returns expected output amount, price impact, and routing information.

quote-eth-usdc.description: Get quote for swapping 1 ETH to USDC on Ethereum mainnet

quote-sepolia-eth-usdc.summary: Quote ETH to USDC on Sepolia
quote-sepolia-eth-usdc.description: Get quote for swapping 0.01 ETH to USDC on Sepolia testnet

400.description: Invalid request or chain not supported

post.description:
Prepares a swap transaction without executing it. Returns serialized transaction data
      that can be used with the /deposit/uniswap/swap endpoint to execute with Turnkey.

      **Important:** This endpoint only PREPARES the transaction. To actually execute the swap,
      you must use the /deposit/uniswap/swap endpoint which will sign and broadcast the transaction
      using Turnkey wallets.

swap-eth-usdc.description: Prepare a swap transaction for 1 ETH to USDC

400.description: Invalid request or chain not supported

get.description: Returns list of chains where Uniswap V4 swaps are supported
Check if the swaps service is operational

message.example: Uniswap V4 Swaps service is running!

post.description: Signs a generic transaction using a wallet account that belongs to the integrator. The transaction must be pre-serialized. Only accounts owned by the integrator associated with the API key can be used.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid input parameters

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

403.description: Forbidden - Account does not belong to this integrator

get.description: Lists signed transactions for the integrator. Can be filtered by status.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of transactions to return (default: 20, max: 100)

post.description: Signs a native token transfer (AVAX, ETH, etc.) transaction. The transaction is built automatically and signed with Turnkey. Only accounts owned by the integrator can be used.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid input or insufficient balance

403.description: Forbidden - Account does not belong to this integrator

post.description: Signs an ERC20 token transfer transaction. The transaction is built automatically and signed with Turnkey. Only accounts owned by the integrator can be used.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid input or insufficient token balance

403.description: Forbidden - Account does not belong to this integrator

get.description: Retrieves details of a signed transaction. Only transactions belonging to the integrator can be accessed.

403.description: Forbidden - Transaction does not belong to this integrator

delete.description: Cancels a pending transaction. Only pending transactions can be cancelled. Only transactions belonging to the integrator can be cancelled.

400.description: Bad Request - Transaction cannot be cancelled

403.description: Forbidden - Transaction does not belong to this integrator

post.description: Broadcasts a previously signed transaction to the blockchain. Only transactions belonging to the integrator can be broadcasted. The transaction must be in pending status and not expired.

400.description: Bad Request - Transaction cannot be broadcasted (expired or wrong status)

403.description: Forbidden - Transaction does not belong to this integrator

get.description: Retrieves the status of a broadcasted transaction including confirmations. Only transactions belonging to the integrator can be accessed.

403.description: Forbidden - Transaction does not belong to this integrator

post.description: Broadcasts a raw signed transaction directly to the blockchain without saving it. Use this for transactions signed outside of this API.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid transaction

post.description: Retries broadcasting a transaction that previously failed. Only failed transactions can be retried. Only transactions belonging to the integrator can be retried.

400.description: Bad Request - Transaction cannot be retried

403.description: Forbidden - Transaction does not belong to this integrator

post.description: Cancels all pending transactions for a wallet address by broadcasting replacement transactions with higher gas prices. This is useful when you hit the maximum pending transactions limit (10) or need to clear stuck transactions. Only wallets owned by the integrator can be used.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid parameters or no pending transactions found

403.description: Forbidden - Wallet does not belong to this integrator

signWith.description: Account to sign with (if from is not provided)

address.description: Wallet address to cancel pending transactions for

chainId.description: Chain ID where the pending transactions are

gasPriceMultiplier.description: Gas price multiplier for replacement transaction (default: 1.2 = 20% more)

transactionHash.description:
Transaction hash if autoBroadcast was true.
      This is the hash of the transaction on the blockchain.
      Use this to track the transaction status on block explorers.

signedTransaction.description:
Signed transaction (hex string) if autoBroadcast was false.
      Use this to manually broadcast the transaction later using the /transactions/broadcast/raw endpoint.

approvalTransactionHash.description:
Transaction hash of the approval transaction (if token approval was required and autoApprove was true).
      Only present for ERC20 token deposits that required approval.

message.example: Deposit transaction signed and broadcasted successfully

signWith.description:
Account identifier to sign the transaction with.
      Can be either:
      - Turnkey Account ID (e.g., "account-123")
      - Ethereum address (e.g., "0x742d35Cc6634C0532925a3b844Bc9e7595f0bEb")
      
      **Important:** The account must belong to the integrator making the request.

tokenAddress.description:
Token address to deposit.
      - For native tokens (AVAX, ETH): Use "0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000" or "avax"
      - For ERC20 tokens: Use the contract address (e.g., USDC on Avalanche: "0xB97EF9Ef8734C71904D8002F8b6Bc66Dd9c48a6E")

amount.description:
Amount to deposit in token units (not wei).
      Use decimal format (e.g., "100.5" for 100.5 USDC).
      The amount will be automatically converted to the correct decimals for the token.

chainId.description:
Chain ID where the deposit will be executed.
      
      **Supported Mainnets (always enabled):**
      - 43114: Avalanche Mainnet
      - 1: Ethereum Mainnet
      - 137: Polygon Mainnet
      - 42161: Arbitrum One
      - 8453: Base Mainnet
      - 10: Optimism Mainnet
      
      **Supported Testnets (enabled if ENABLE_TESTNETS=true):**
      - 43113: Avalanche Fuji Testnet
      - 5: Ethereum Goerli Testnet
      - 11155111: Ethereum Sepolia Testnet
      - 80001: Polygon Mumbai Testnet
      - 421613: Arbitrum Goerli Testnet
      - 84531: Base Goerli Testnet
      - 420: Optimism Goerli Testnet
      
      **Note:** Testnets are controlled by the `ENABLE_TESTNETS` environment variable.
      By default, testnets are enabled in development/staging and disabled in production.
      
      **Validation:** This chain must be supported by AAVE SDK. The validation happens before any external API calls.

autoApprove.description:
Automatically approve token spending if required (for ERC20 tokens).
      If true and approval is needed, the approval transaction will be signed and broadcasted automatically.
      Default: true
      
      **Note:** For native tokens (AVAX, ETH), this parameter is ignored.

autoBroadcast.description:
Automatically broadcast the signed transaction to the blockchain.
      - If true: Transaction is signed and immediately broadcasted. Returns transactionHash.
      - If false: Transaction is signed but not broadcasted. Returns signedTransaction for manual broadcast.
      
      Default: false
      
      **Use cases:**
      - autoBroadcast=true: For automated deposits where you want immediate execution
      - autoBroadcast=false: For manual review before broadcasting or batch operations

signWith.description: Account identifier to sign the transaction with

amount.description: Amount to withdraw in token units (decimal format)

chainId.description:
Chain ID where the withdrawal will be executed.
      
      **Note:** This chain must be supported by AAVE SDK. The validation happens before any external API calls.

approvalTransactionHash.description: Approval transaction hash (if approval was needed)

message.example: Swap transaction signed and broadcasted successfully

signWith.description: Wallet account ID or address to sign the transaction with

tokenIn.description: Input token address (use 0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 for native token)

amountIn.description: Amount to swap (in token decimals, as string)

autoApprove.description: Automatically approve token if needed (default: true)

autoBroadcast.description: Automatically broadcast the transaction after signing (default: true)

deadline.description: Deadline timestamp (optional, defaults to 20 minutes from now)
